subject: English
title: Life Around Us

sections.heading: Life Around Us
sections.content:
54 Mridang
Unit 4
Life Around Us
Chapter 1
A Show of Clouds 
Let us recite
As I lie on my back,
Looking up at the sky.
I see a big white bear,
Looking down at me.
As I lie on my back,
Looking up at the sky.
I see a ship sailing,
On a white sea.
Reprint 2026-27
55Mridang
As I lie on my back,
Looking up at the sky.
I see an elephant,
Waving its trunk at me.
As I lie on my back,
Looking up at the sky.
Oh! It’s fun, it’s fun to me,
To see whatever I want to see.
Reprint 2026-27
56 Mridang
A. Say aloud
1. Sit outside your home, or in an open space and watch
the clouds go by. Next day, tell the class what you
saw.
2. What are the colours of clouds? Tick the colours that
clouds can be.
Let us speak
Note to the teacher
• Let the children speak. Encourage them to speak in English.
• Accept answers even if they respond in only one word or a phrase.
• Ask children to explain. For instance, if children say ‘The cloud
is red’, they may be thinking of clouds at sunset.
New words
lie | bear | sea | sailing | waving |
Sight words
as | up | at | my |sky | back |
white
red
black
grey
green
blue
Reprint 2026-27
57Mridang
B. Answer the questions asked by the teacher.
Note to the teacher
• All answers are acceptable, real or imaginary.
• Provide regular practice of the above sentence structures.
• Help children answer in full sentences.
Teacher: When you look up in the sky, what do you see?
Child 1: When I look up, I see _ _ _
Teacher: When you look down at the ground, what do
you see?
Child 2: When I look down, I see _ _ _
Teacher: When you close your eyes, what do you see?
Child 3: When I close my eyes, I see _ _ _
Reprint 2026-27
58 Mridang
A. Answer the following questions.
1. In the poem, what animals did the child see as he
looked up at the clouds?
The child could see ________________________________
2. What do you see in the sky during the day?
Write any three things you see.
____________________________________________________
____________________________________________________
____________________________________________________
3. What do you see in the sky at night?
____________________________________________________
____________________________________________________
Let us write
Reprint 2026-27
59Mridang
Picture time
A. Join the dots in alphabetical order and colour the
picture.
Let us draw
Reprint 2026-27
60 Mridang
Let us listen
Note to the teacher
• Let the children repeat the above words, one by one, noticing the
‘ck’ sound.
• The children need not know the meaning of these words.
What sound is common in these words?
A. Repeat after the teacher.
The poem has the word ‘back’.
Listen to your teacher and say the following words, with
your teacher.
back
lackpack jack rack
tickpick flick thick
duckpluck muck truck
Reprint 2026-27

learningObjectives: Understand the key concepts of Life Around Us
Identify and explain the main ideas covered in this chapter
Apply knowledge from this chapter to real-world situations
Recall important terms and definitions from Life Around Us
